name: Dearg Due
description: The Dearg Due (Irish for 'Red Blood Sucker') is a female vampire-like creature in Irish mythology. She is said to lure men to their deaths with her beauty and then drain their blood. Legends say she was once a beautiful woman forced into a marriage against her will to a cruel and wealthy man. Consumed by hatred and despair, she made a pact with a demon or other dark entity to gain power and exact revenge on her oppressor and all men. She is often depicted as having long, flowing red hair, sharp teeth, and a thirst for blood. Some tales describe her as being imprisoned in a grave or cairn, only to emerge at night to hunt. The Dearg Due is a cautionary tale about the dangers of forced marriage and the destructive power of vengeance. She embodies the dark side of female power and the seductive allure of the supernatural. Her legend serves as a warning against succumbing to bitterness and seeking revenge at any cost. Encounters with the Dearg Due are always fatal, as she leaves her victims drained and lifeless.
subtitle: The Irish Red Blood Sucker
appearance: The Dearg Due is described as a strikingly beautiful woman with long, flowing red hair, pale skin, and piercing eyes. Her most notable features are her sharp, pointed teeth, which she uses to pierce the throats of her victims. She is often depicted wearing a long, flowing gown, typically red or black, which conceals her unnatural movements and features. Some accounts mention her possessing unnaturally long fingernails, used for defense and attack.
habitat: The Dearg Due is said to inhabit remote, rural areas of Ireland, particularly ancient burial grounds, cairns, and old ruins. She is most active at night, preying on unsuspecting travelers who venture into her territory. Some legends place her near specific historical sites associated with tragic events or injustice.
behavior: The Dearg Due is a predatory creature driven by a thirst for blood and a desire for revenge. She uses her beauty and charm to lure men into vulnerable positions before attacking and draining them of their life force. She is cunning and manipulative, exploiting the weaknesses and desires of her victims. She is said to be particularly drawn to men who resemble her former oppressor, seeking to punish them for his crimes. The Dearg Due is a solitary creature, rarely interacting with other supernatural beings.

keyMyths.title: The Forced Marriage
keyMyths.description: A beautiful woman was forced to marry a cruel and wealthy man against her will. Driven to despair, she made a pact with a dark entity to gain power and seek revenge.

keyMyths.title: The Red Grave
keyMyths.description: The Dearg Due is often said to be buried alive in a grave or cairn, only to emerge at night to hunt for blood. Her resting place is often marked by red stains or an eerie presence.

sources.author: W.B. Yeats
sources.work: Fairy and Folk Tales of the Irish Peasantry
sources.description: A collection of Irish folklore, including mentions of vampire-like creatures.
sources.text: A collection of Irish folklore, including mentions of vampire-like creatures.

sources.author: Lady Wilde
sources.work: Ancient Legends, Mystic Charms, and Superstitions of Ireland
sources.description: Provides insights into Irish superstitions and beliefs about supernatural beings.
